description: Structural census of the 280 operational bridge theorems (results/bridge_theorems.json; PURITY>=0.50, premises spanning >=2 pure clusters) and the strict 34 (depth-filtered) vs the full population of 55,575 theorems-with-tactics-and-premises. Four pure-stdlib features per theorem from data/theorem_tactics.json + data/tactic_steps.jsonl: proof_len (#tactic steps), n_premises (distinct out-premises), reuse_indeg (#distinct theorems using it as a premise), dag_depth (longest premise-dependency chain below it). Report medians, means, and rankfrac=P(bridge feature > random population theorem; 0.5=no diff).
motivation: a-0043 showed the detection half's 280 operational bridges are a tiny (~0.2-0.3%), near-disjoint population from the generic cross-domain theorems the q-0011 retrieval chain measures, but the paper never characterizes WHAT KIND of theorem a bridge is. Are operational bridges deep syntheses (long, premise-heavy, downstream, rarely reused) or foundational connectors (short, shallow, heavily reused)? Cheap CPU-only census over existing data.
formal_results: Population 55,575 theorems (tactics+premises). proof_len: pop median 2 / mean 4.50; bridges(280) median 10 / mean 15.02 (rankfrac 0.785); strict(34) median 12 / mean 19.74 (0.851). n_premises: pop median 3 / mean 5.57; bridges median 12 / mean 17.22 (0.817); strict median 16 / mean 21.85 (0.887). reuse_indeg: pop median 0 / mean 1.36; bridges median 1 / mean 1.04 (0.532); strict median 1 / mean 1.09 (0.532). dag_depth: pop median 2 / mean 4.20; bridges median 3 / mean 6.06 (0.611); strict median 6.5 / mean 9.53 (0.789).
results_description: Operational bridges are markedly LONGER (5-6x the median proof length) and far more PREMISE-HEAVY (4-5x the median premise count) than typical theorems, and sit DOWNSTREAM in the dependency DAG (especially the strict 34: depth rankfrac 0.789). But they are NOT more REUSED than the typical theorem (reuse_indeg rankfrac 0.532, barely above chance; bridge mean 1.04 is actually below the population mean 1.36 inflated by foundational hubs).
conclusions: Operational bridge theorems are deep SYNTHESES, not foundational connector lemmas: late-stage, long, premise-heavy results that pull together many specialized premises across domains, while themselves being rarely reused. This is the structural complement of the q-0007 depth filter, which deliberately strips the high-in-degree utility hubs - so bridges are the opposite end of the reuse spectrum. It mechanistically explains a-0043's near-disjoint-populations finding: the detected bridges are big downstream syntheses, structurally unlike the generic cross-domain test theorems and unlike reused utility lemmas.
